task_completion.description: Create a saved SQL query named 'GenreRevenue'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In at least one screenshot, look for 'GenreRevenue' listed in the Queries pane of the LibreOffice Base main window, or an open query design/SQL window with this name showing a join between InvoiceLine, Track, and Genre.

task_completion.description: Create a saved SQL query named 'CountryRevenue'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In at least one screenshot, look for 'CountryRevenue' listed in the Queries pane of the LibreOffice Base main window, or an open query design/SQL window with this name showing a join between Invoice and Customer.

task_completion.description: Create a new table named 'RevenueTarget' with specific columns
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'RevenueTarget' table in the Tables pane, or an open table design/data window showing the columns: TargetId, Genre, AnnualTarget, and Quarter.

task_completion.description: Insert at least four rows into the RevenueTarget table with realistic genres
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot showing the Data View of the 'RevenueTarget' table with at least 4 rows populated. The 'Genre' column should contain realistic music genres (e.g., Rock, Jazz, Classical, Pop).
Look for 'Revenue Analysis' listed in the Reports pane of the LibreOffice Base main window, or a screenshot of the generated report window displaying genre revenue data.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Base GUI to complete the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the LibreOffice Base interface (menus, dialogs, query/table/report editors) rather than terminal commands modifying the .odb file directly.

integrity.description: The agent worked within the chinook.odb file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The title bar of the LibreOffice Base main window should indicate 'chinook.odb' or 'chinook'.

integrity.description: The agent manually entered or properly generated the table data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data in the RevenueTarget table should look like distinct rows with different TargetIds, Genres, and numerical targets, not a single pasted block of text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the Chinook database, a real music store sample database. The 'GenreRevenue' query must join InvoiceLine, Track, and Genre tables. The 'CountryRevenue' query must join Invoice and Customer tables. The 'RevenueTarget' table must have columns: TargetId (integer, PK), Genre (text), AnnualTarget (numeric), and Quarter (integer). It must contain at least 4 rows with realistic genres (e.g., Rock, Jazz, Classical, Pop). The report must be named 'Revenue Analysis'. All work must be saved in chinook.odb.
